original_hypothesis.title: De-whitelisting a Market Permanently Freezes User Rewards
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If governance de-whitelists a market, the update_market function will revert. Because claim() calls update_market(), users are permanently prevented from claiming rewards they already earned prior to the de-whitelisting.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Administrative actions should not confiscate legitimately earned user funds. The lack of a grace period or emergency claim path means a routine governance action will unintentionally harm users.
preconditions: Users have unclaimed rewards in that market

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Strict access control blocking withdrawal paths
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: No bypass for claiming past rewards when a market is deactivated

analysis_questions: Is there any alternative path for users to recover their rewards?
Does de-whitelisting also freeze withdrawals on the lending market side?
validation_requirements: Simulate a de-whitelisting event and verify that all claim attempts revert

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governance decides to deprecate an old lending market and de-whitelists it. Immediately, all users who haven't claimed their CANTO rewards lose access to them. If the lending market calls sync_ledger on withdrawal, users also cannot withdraw their underlying deposits.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of earned rewards and potentially frozen underlying deposits
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users in the deprecated market
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Governance must re-whitelist the market to allow claims

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for user claims after market de-whitelisting

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
